content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 21, 1989


Adolph Donins
1931 North H Street, #67
Oxnard, CA 93030

Re:  Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-89-682

Dear Mr. Donins:

     You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").1/  Your request is
one for general assistance.  Accordingly, we are treating it as a
request for informal assistance.2/

QUESTIONS

     Which employees of the State Compensation Insurance Fund (the
"Fund") are required to file statements of economic interests?
Where are those statements located, and are they of public record?

CONCLUSION

     The conflict of interest code for the Fund is currently on file
at the offices of the Fair Political Practices Commission.  As stated
in the conflict of interest code, the original financial disclosure
statements for the board of directors are forwarded to our offices.
However, original statements for all additional designated positions,
and copies of the board of directors' statements, are filed at the
Fund's offices in Sacramento.  Pursuant to Section 81008, every
statement of economic interests filed pursuant to the Act is a public
record and open for public inspection and reproduction.

_____________________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000,
et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of
the California Code of Regulations.

2/  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Mr. Donins
December 21, 1989
Page 2


I have enclosed a copy of the conflict of interest code for
the Fund. To obtain copies of statements of economic interests
for designated employees, you should contact the agency directly
at its Sacramento office. If you are interested only in the board
of director's statements, you may contact our Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662 for assistance.

FACTS

You have been referred to the offices of the Fair Political
Practices Commission to ascertain if the employees of the State
Compensation Insurance Fund are filing disclosure statements, and
if so, whether you can obtain copies from our office.

ANALYSIS

Conflict of interest codes must be promulgated by all state
agencies. They must designate those positions within the agency
which make or participate in the making of decisions which may
have a foreseeable material financial effect on any financial
interest. For each designated position, the code must describe
the types of financial interests made reportable.

To determine which employees are designated and what
disclosure requirements they have been assigned, please refer to
the enclosed copy of the conflict of interest code for the Fund,
complete with the appendix of designated employees and the
appendix of disclosure categories.

I hope that the above has been helpful to you. If you have
any questions, or would like to discuss this further, please feel
free to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel


By: Cheryl A. Hoff
Staff Services Analyst
Legal Division

KED:CAH:I89682

cc  State Compensation Insurance Fund

Fair Politics Practice Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca 95804

Nov 29 | 1:17 PM '89

Dear Sirs, Madams,

My name is Adolph Donins, and I'm representing myself
in a Workers' Compensation case. ( Case No. 87 P 10301). The name of Insurance Company in the case is- State Compensation Insurance Fund,669 County
Square Drive, Ventura, Ca 93002. The private agency or company involved in
this case is- DS Associates- Rehabilitation Consultants, 5755 Valentine
Road, Suite 304, Ventura, Ca 93003.

My complaint is that the owners of DS Associates-
Terry Dinneen, Susan Okoneski and Pamela Joyce- have spouses working for
State Fund, Ventura branch in the capacity of Sales Manager and Assistant
Claim Manager respectively. Therefore, in my judgment there's a violation -
California Government Code 3625. Auditors General Office . with whom I'm
handling the case, suggested that the State Fund
has "file Disclosure relating to financial interests , as far as the owners/
spouses of DS Associates are concerned.

When I contacted the legal department of State Fund
I was told that this information is confidential, but that 'designed employees' of State Fund do file disclosure which is found with your agency -
and is public record. Therefore, please write me , informing that there's
disclosure done and filled with you, or , there's no disclosure.

Sincerely,

[signature: adolph donins]

Adolph Donins
1931 N. Hill St. # 67
Oxnard, Ca 93030
Nov. 27, 1989
1-805-4855242

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 1, 1989

Adolph Donins
1931 N. "H" Street, #67
Oxnard, CA 93030

Re: Letter No. 89-682

Dear Mr. Donins:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on November 29, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Cheryl Hoff, in the Legal Division, directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you
as to the information needed. If your request is for informal
assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See
Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
